name: pydantic_schemas_audit

analysis.optional_fields_without_default: FALSE POSITIVES - SQLAlchemy ORM objects always have all attributes (even NULL), so Pydantic validation with from_attributes=True succeeds. Adding default=None is recommended for best practices but NOT required.
analysis.extra_forbid: MEDIUM - Request schemas without extra='forbid' will silently ignore extra fields. This is acceptable but less strict validation.

issues.impact: Extra fields silently ignored - acceptable but less strict

false_positives.reason: Pydantic v2 with from_attributes=True works correctly with SQLAlchemy ORM objects. SQLAlchemy always provides all attributes (even NULL), so Optional fields without default=None work correctly.
false_positives.test_result: Verified: TestResponse.model_validate(obj with None attr) returns SUCCESS

notes: 6 Request schemas without extra='forbid' - MEDIUM priority to add for stricter validation
14 Optional field issues are FALSE POSITIVES - SQLAlchemy ORM integration works correctly
Pydantic v2 from_attributes=True correctly handles None values from SQLAlchemy
Recommendation: Add default=None to Optional fields for best practices and consistency
No CRITICAL or HIGH severity issues found in Pydantic schemas
